LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER PROTECT OUR BENEFITS'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 20, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE BOTH PARTIES THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6C. BOTH PARTIES MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE PETITION AND ANSWER TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 21, 2013 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE NOTICE, OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-20-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: PETITIONER PROTECT OUR BENEFITS' PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. PETITIONER HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE COMPELLING THE CONCLUSION THAT SUBSECTION (D) IS UNREASONABLE AND INVALID. (CITY OF SAN DIEGO V. HAAS (2012) 207 CAL.APP.4TH 472, 496.) PETITIONER'S ARGUMENT THAT SUBSECTION (D) IS AN IMPAIRMENT ON CONTRACT BECAUSE IT ESSENTIALLY PROHIBITS SUPPLEMENTAL COLAS TO RETIREES BECAUSE SUPPLEMENTAL COLAS HAVE BEEN ISSUED SINCE 1996 IS FLAWED. PETITIONER CONCEDES THAT "RETIREES DID NOT RECEIVE SUPPLEMENTAL COLAS IN 1996, 2003, 2008 AND 2009." (POINTS & AUTH., P. 4:4-5. SEE REQ. JUD. NOTICE, EXHIBIT C.) FURTHER, SUPPLEMENTAL COLAS HAVE HISTORICALLY BEEN SUBJECT TO CONDITIONS. (CITY CHARTER A8.526-1(A) & (B).) SUBSECTION (D) DOES NOT ABROGATE THE SPIRIT OF THESE PRIOR AMENDMENTS. (UNITED FIREFIGHTERS OF LOS ANGELES CITY V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (1989) 210 CAL.APP.3D 1095, 1102-03.) PETITIONER HAS NOT PROVIDED SUFFICIENT LEGAL AUTHORITY TO SUPPORT THE PROPOSITION THAT THE COURT MAY QUESTION THE ACCURACY OF LANGUAGE CONTAINED IN A VOTER-APPROVED INITIATIVE AND INVALIDATE IT ON THIS BASIS. FURTHER, " 'THE VOTERS ARE PRESUMED TO BE AWARE OF EXISTING LAW [CITATION], INCLUDING THE ADMINISTRATIVE ENFORCEMENT PROVISIONS. [CITATIONS.]" (SACKS V. CITY OF OAKLAND (2010) 190 CAL.APP.4TH 1070, 1085.) LASTLY, PETITIONER HAS NOT PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT THE ACTUARIAL REPORTS PRESENTED TO THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS BY CHEIRON DO NOT COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE CITY CHARTER.COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF REQUESTS A STATEMENT OF DECISION PER CCP SECTION 632. COUNSEL FOR BOTH PARTIES TO SUBMIT PROPOSED STATEMENTS OF DECISION BY AUGUST 20, 2013 TO DEPARTMENT 303. COUNSEL IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT BOTH A HARD COPY AND AN ELECTRONIC COPY OF PROPOSED STATEMENT OF DECISION. COUNSEL IS ALSO ORDERED TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THIS RULING. JUDGE: R.A. KRAM

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Jul-24-2015 Single Assignment

Petitioner Protect Our Benefits' Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DAVID CLISHAM, ESQ., 415.291.0855, AND JUSTINE CLISHAM, ESQ., 212.317.0951, FOR PLAINTIFF PROTECT OUR BENEFITS.
WAYNE SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.4700, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: Petitioner Protect Our Benefits' Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Petitioner has not presented evidence compelling the conclusion that Subsection (d) is unreasonable and invalid. (City of San Diego v. Haas (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 472, 496.) Petitioner's argument that Subsection (d) is an impairment on contract because it essentially prohibits supplemental COLAs to retirees because supplemental COLAs have been issued since 1996 is flawed. Petitioner concedes that "retirees did not receive supplemental COLAs in 1996, 2003, 2008 and 2009." (Points & Auth., p. 4:4-5. See Req. Jud. Notice, Exhibit C.) Further, supplemental COLAs have historically been subject to conditions. (City Charter A8.526-1(a) & (b).) Subsection (d) does not abrogate the spirit of these prior amendments. (United Firefighters of Los Angeles City v. City of Los Angeles (1989) 210 Cal.App.3d 1095, 1102-03.) Petitioner has not provided sufficient legal authority to support the proposition that the Court may question the accuracy of language contained in a voter-approved initiative and invalidate it on this basis. Further, " 'The voters are presumed to be aware of existing law [citation], including the administrative enforcement provisions. [Citations.]" (Sacks v. City of Oakland (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 1070, 1085.) Lastly, Petitioner has not presented sufficient evidence to show that the actuarial reports presented to the Board of Supervisors by Cheiron do not comply with the requirements of the City Charter.COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF REQUESTS A STATEMENT OF DECISION PER CCP SECTION 632. COUNSEL FOR BOTH PARTIES TO SUBMIT PROPOSED STATEMENTS OF DECISION BY AUGUST 20, 2013 TO DEPARTMENT 303. COUNSEL IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT BOTH A HARD COPY AND AN ELECTRONIC COPY OF PROPOSED STATEMENT OF DECISION. COUNSEL IS ALSO ORDERED TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THIS RULING. Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.
